Tonnetz vs Chroma vs PCA for Automatic Chord Estimation¶

Eight experimental conditions: architecture and parameter count held constant, only input representation varies

Model Input Dim Description
CNN-chroma librosa CQT chroma 12 Standard hand-crafted baseline
CNN-deep_chroma madmom DeepChromaProcessor 12 Learned denoised chroma
CNN-tonnetz Tonnetz from CQT chroma 6 Theory-driven harmonic projection
CNN-tonnetz_deep Tonnetz from DeepChroma 6 Denoised Tonnetz
CNN-chroma_pca6 PCA on CQT chroma 6 Data-driven projection (CQT)
CNN-deep_chroma_pca6 PCA on DeepChroma 6 Data-driven projection (deep)
CNN-chroma_rand6 Random orthonormal proj. on CQT 6 Null baseline (CQT)
CNN-deep_chroma_rand6 Random orthonormal proj. on deep 6 Null baseline (deep)

Three corpora spanning synth, pop, and classical:

  • IDMT SMT-Chords — clean synthesised chords (guitar + non-guitar)
  • Beatles — Revolver & Sgt Pepper's (real-world pop)
  • Schubert Winterreise — solo piano, chromatic Romantic harmony (SC06 performance)

Seven transfer experiments (E1–E7):

  • E1–E2: SMT ↔ Beatles (synth ↔ pop)
  • E3–E4: SMT ↔ Schubert (synth ↔ classical)
  • E5–E6: SMT ↔ All real (synth ↔ mixed)
  • E7: Mixed holdout — cross-genre generalisation

Evaluation: Accuracy, Macro/Micro/Weighted F1, TWE (Tonnetz-distance error), MCE (consonance error), error decomposition (root vs quality vs both wrong).

Result: The optimal representation depends on harmonic complexity. CNN-deep_chroma_pca6 wins on diatonic pop (Beatles), but CNN-tonnetz_deep dominates on chromatic classical music (Schubert) and cross-genre transfer (6 of 7 experiments overall). The Tonnetz geometry provides a genuine advantage for harmonically complex music.

1. Setup¶

In [86]:
!pip install librosa madmom soundfile --quiet
In [87]:
# ── Cell 2: Import everything EXCEPT madmom first ─────────────────────────────
import os, re, warnings
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import librosa
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from pathlib import Path
from tqdm.notebook import tqdm
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader, random_split
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
SEED = 42
torch.manual_seed(SEED)
torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(SEED)
np.random.seed(SEED)
DEVICE = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
print(f'Device: {DEVICE}')
torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = True
torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = False

Device: cuda
In [88]:
# ── Cell 3: madmom compatibility patches — run AFTER all other imports ─────────
import collections, collections.abc

for _name in ['MutableSequence', 'MutableMapping', 'Callable', 'Mapping',
              'Sequence', 'Iterator', 'Iterable', 'MutableSet']:
    if not hasattr(collections, _name):
        setattr(collections, _name, getattr(collections.abc, _name))

import numpy as _np
for _name, _type in [('float', float), ('int', int), ('complex', complex),
                     ('bool', bool), ('object', object), ('str', str)]:
    if not hasattr(_np, _name):
        setattr(_np, _name, _type)

# ── NOW it's safe to import madmom ────────────────────────────────────────────
import madmom

2. Configuration¶

In [89]:
# ── Environment detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ON_KAGGLE = Path('/kaggle').exists()

if ON_KAGGLE:
    SMT_ROOT     = Path('/kaggle/input/datasets/zburkett44/smt-chords')
    BEATLES_ROOT = Path('/kaggle/input/datasets/zburkett44/chords')
    FEAT_DIR     = Path('/kaggle/working/features')
    OUT_DIR      = Path('/kaggle/working')
    BEATLES_META_PATHS = [
    BEATLES_ROOT / 'revolver_chords'  / 'revolver_chords'    / 'metadata.csv',
    BEATLES_ROOT / 'sgt_peppers_chords' / 'sgt_peppers_chords' / 'metadata.csv',
    ]
    SCHUBERT_ROOT = Path('/kaggle/input/datasets/ollyflaig/schubert-winterreise-chordwise')
else:
    _HERE        = Path.cwd()
    SMT_ROOT     = _HERE / 'IDMT-SMT-CHORDS'
    BEATLES_ROOT = _HERE / 'beatles'
    FEAT_DIR     = _HERE / 'features'
    OUT_DIR      = _HERE / 'features'
        
    BEATLES_META_PATHS = [
        BEATLES_ROOT / 'revolver_chords'    / 'metadata.csv',
        BEATLES_ROOT / 'sgt_peppers_chords' / 'metadata.csv',
    ]
    SCHUBERT_ROOT = _HERE / 'schubert-winterreise-chordwise'


FEAT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
OUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
print(f'Running on Kaggle: {ON_KAGGLE}')
print(f'SMT_ROOT:     {SMT_ROOT}')
print(f'BEATLES_ROOT: {BEATLES_ROOT}')
print(f'FEAT_DIR:     {FEAT_DIR}')
print(f'OUT_DIR:      {OUT_DIR}')

# ── Vocabulary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
QUALITIES    = ['maj', 'min']
ROOTS        = ['C','Cs','D','Ds','E','F','Fs','G','Gs','A','As','B']
CHORD_LABELS = [f'{r}:{q}' for r in ROOTS for q in QUALITIES]   # 24 classes
N_CLASSES    = len(CHORD_LABELS)
LABEL2IDX    = {l: i for i, l in enumerate(CHORD_LABELS)}
IDX2LABEL    = {i: l for l, i in LABEL2IDX.items()}

# Root name normalisation: SMT uses C#, Beatles metadata may also vary
ROOT_NORM = {
    'C#':'Cs','D#':'Ds','F#':'Fs','G#':'Gs','A#':'As',
    'Db':'Cs','Eb':'Ds','Gb':'Fs','Ab':'Gs','Bb':'As',
}

# ── Audio / feature settings ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
SR       = 22050
HOP      = 512
N_FRAMES = 32     # fixed time axis — all segments padded/truncated to this

# ── Training hyperparameters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
BATCH_SIZE = 64
EPOCHS     = 40
LR         = 1e-3
VAL_SPLIT  = 0.2

# ── Colour palette (consistent across all plots) ───────────────────────────────
COLORS = {
    'chroma':       '#4C72B0',
    'deep_chroma':  '#55A868',
    'tonnetz':      '#DD8452',
    'tonnetz_deep': '#C44E52',
}

REP_DIMS = {'chroma':12, 'deep_chroma':12, 'tonnetz':6, 'tonnetz_deep':6}

print(f'Vocabulary: {N_CLASSES} classes')
print(CHORD_LABELS)

['C:maj', 'C:min', 'Cs:maj', 'Cs:min', 'D:maj', 'D:min', 'Ds:maj', 'Ds:min', 'E:maj', 'E:min', 'F:maj', 'F:min', 'Fs:maj', 'Fs:min', 'G:maj', 'G:min', 'Gs:maj', 'Gs:min', 'A:maj', 'A:min', 'As:maj', 'As:min', 'B:maj', 'B:min']

3. Data Loading¶

3a. IDMT SMT-Chords (train/val)¶

Layout: One folder per instrument group (guitar/, non_guitar/). Each folder contains multiple .wav files (one per instrument/sampler) and a single shared .lab annotation file. The .lab lists start_time end_time chord_label at 2-second intervals covering all 7 chord types × 12 roots × inversions × 2 repetitions. We parse the .lab, filter to maj/min, strip inversions, and pair each annotation with every wav in the folder.

In [90]:
SMT_ROOT_MAP = {
    'C':'C','C#':'Cs','D':'D','D#':'Ds','E':'E','F':'F',
    'F#':'Fs','G':'G','G#':'Gs','A':'A','A#':'As','B':'B',
    'Db':'Cs','Eb':'Ds','Gb':'Fs','Ab':'Gs','Bb':'As',
}

def parse_lab_file(lab_path: Path) -> list:
    """Parse .lab annotation file → list of {start, end, label} dicts.
    Strips inversion suffix (C:maj/3 → C:maj), filters to maj/min."""
    segments = []
    with open(lab_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.strip().split()
            if len(parts) < 3:
                continue
            start, end, chord = float(parts[0]), float(parts[1]), parts[2]
            chord_base = chord.split('/')[0]        # strip inversion
            if ':' not in chord_base:
                continue
            root_raw, quality = chord_base.split(':', 1)
            if quality not in QUALITIES:
                continue
            root = SMT_ROOT_MAP.get(root_raw)
            if root is None:
                continue
            label = f'{root}:{quality}'
            if label not in LABEL2IDX:
                continue
            segments.append({'start': start, 'end': end, 'label': label})
    return segments


def load_smt_metadata(smt_root: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Walk SMT directory, pair each annotation with every wav in the same folder.
    Returns DataFrame: filepath | start | end | label | family"""
    records = []
    for lab_path in sorted(smt_root.rglob('*.lab')):
        folder  = lab_path.parent
        wavs    = sorted(folder.glob('*.wav'))
        segments = parse_lab_file(lab_path)
        family  = 'guitar' if 'non_guitar' not in str(folder) and 'guitar' in str(folder) else 'non_guitar'
        for wav in wavs:
            for seg in segments:
                records.append({
                    'filepath': str(wav),
                    'start':    seg['start'],
                    'end':      seg['end'],
                    'label':    seg['label'],
                    'family':   family,
                })
    df = pd.DataFrame(records)
    print(f'SMT-Chords: {len(df)} segments across {df["filepath"].nunique()} wavs')
    print(df.groupby('family')['label'].count())
    return df


smt_df = load_smt_metadata(SMT_ROOT)
smt_df.head()

SMT-Chords: 1908 segments across 16 wavs
family
guitar         468
non_guitar    1440

3b. Beatles (test)¶

Pre-segmented .wav files organised by root note. Two albums: Revolver and Sgt. Pepper's. Labels from metadata.csv.

In [91]:
def load_beatles_metadata(beatles_root: Path, meta_paths: list) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Load Beatles segments from metadata CSVs.
    Filters to maj/min, normalises root names, resolves full filepaths."""
    frames = []
    for meta_path in meta_paths:
        if not meta_path.exists():
            print(f'Warning: not found: {meta_path}')
            continue
        meta  = pd.read_csv(meta_path)
        meta  = meta[meta['quality'].isin(QUALITIES)].copy()
        # Normalise root names (C# → Cs, Db → Cs etc.)
        meta['root'] = meta['root'].replace(ROOT_NORM)
        meta  = meta[meta['root'].isin(ROOTS)].copy()
        meta['label'] = meta['root'] + ':' + meta['quality']
        meta  = meta[meta['label'].isin(LABEL2IDX)].copy()
        # Determine album from path
        album = 'revolver' if 'revolver' in str(meta_path).lower() else 'sgt_peppers'
        meta['album'] = album
        # Resolve absolute filepath
        album_dir = meta_path.parent
        def resolve(fp):
            fp_clean = re.sub(r'_(root|inv)', '', str(fp))
            candidate = album_dir / fp_clean
            if candidate.exists():
                return str(candidate)
            matches = list(album_dir.rglob(Path(fp_clean).name))
            return str(matches[0]) if matches else None
        meta['filepath'] = meta['filepath'].apply(resolve)
        meta = meta.dropna(subset=['filepath'])
        frames.append(meta[['filepath','label','root','quality','album']])
    df = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    print(f'Beatles: {len(df)} segments (maj/min only)')
    print(df.groupby('album')['label'].count())
    return df


beatles_df = load_beatles_metadata(BEATLES_ROOT, BEATLES_META_PATHS)
beatles_df.head()

4 /kaggle/input/datasets/zburkett44/chords/revol... C:maj C maj revolver

3c. Schubert Winterreise (additional real-world test)¶

Pre-segmented .wav files from the Schubert Winterreise dataset, organised by performance/piece/root/quality. Multiple performances available — we use SC06 as the primary performance and split by piece number for train/test within Schubert.

Key difference from Beatles: classical solo piano, chromatic Romantic harmony (chromatic mediants, enharmonic pivots, third-relations) vs. diatonic pop. This tests whether representations that work for pop transfer to a fundamentally different harmonic language.

In [92]:
def load_schubert_metadata(schubert_root: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Load Schubert Winterreise segments from directory structure.
    Structure: performance/piece/root/quality/*.wav
    Applies root normalisation and filters to 24-class maj/min vocabulary."""
    records = []
    for root_dir, dirs, files in schubert_root.walk(on_error=print):
        for file in files:
            if Path(file).suffix == '.wav':
                metadata = [x.stem for x in (Path(f"{root_dir}/{file}")).parents]
                quality, root_note, piece, performance = metadata[:4]
                # Normalise root names (C# → Cs, Db → Cs, etc.)
                root_note = ROOT_NORM.get(root_note, root_note)
                if root_note not in ROOTS or quality not in QUALITIES:
                    continue
                label = f'{root_note}:{quality}'
                if label not in LABEL2IDX:
                    continue
                records.append({
                    'filepath':    f"{root_dir}/{file}",
                    'piece':       int(piece),
                    'label':       label,
                    'root':        root_note,
                    'quality':     quality,
                    'performance': performance,
                })
    df = pd.DataFrame(records)
    print(f'Schubert Winterreise: {len(df)} segments total')
    print(f'Performances: {sorted(df["performance"].unique())}')
    print(f'Pieces: {df["piece"].nunique()}, Roots: {df["root"].nunique()}')
    print(df.groupby('performance')['label'].count())
    return df


schubert_df_all = load_schubert_metadata(SCHUBERT_ROOT)

# Use SC06 as the primary performance
SCHUBERT_PERF = 'SC06'
schubert_df = schubert_df_all[schubert_df_all['performance'] == SCHUBERT_PERF].copy()
schubert_df = schubert_df.reset_index(drop=True)

# Split by piece number: first half train, second half test
no_pieces = schubert_df['piece'].max()
train_split = 0.5
piece_cutoff = int(train_split * no_pieces)
schubert_train_mask_df = schubert_df['piece'] <= piece_cutoff
schubert_test_mask_df  = schubert_df['piece'] > piece_cutoff

schubert_train_df = schubert_df[schubert_train_mask_df].reset_index(drop=True)
schubert_test_df  = schubert_df[schubert_test_mask_df].reset_index(drop=True)

print(f'\nUsing performance: {SCHUBERT_PERF}')
print(f'Total: {len(schubert_df)}  Train (pieces 1-{piece_cutoff}): {len(schubert_train_df)}  '
      f'Test (pieces {piece_cutoff+1}-{no_pieces}): {len(schubert_test_df)}')
print(f'\nClass distribution (SC06):')
print(schubert_df['label'].value_counts().head(10))

3d. EDA — class balance¶

In [93]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(20, 4))
from matplotlib.patches import Patch

for ax, df, title in zip(axes,
                         [smt_df,     beatles_df,     schubert_df],
                         ['SMT-Chords (train/val)', 'Beatles (test)', f'Schubert ({SCHUBERT_PERF})']):
    counts = df['label'].value_counts().reindex(CHORD_LABELS, fill_value=0)
    bar_colors = ['#4C72B0' if 'maj' in l else '#DD8452' for l in counts.index]
    ax.bar(range(len(counts)), counts.values, color=bar_colors)
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(counts)))
    ax.set_xticklabels(counts.index, rotation=90, fontsize=7)
    ax.set_title(title, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_ylabel('Count')
    ax.legend(handles=[Patch(color='#4C72B0',label='major'),
                       Patch(color='#DD8452',label='minor')])

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'class_balance.png'), dpi=150)
plt.show()

# Print Schubert coverage
missing = [l for l in CHORD_LABELS if l not in schubert_df['label'].values]
if missing:
    print(f'Warning: Schubert ({SCHUBERT_PERF}) missing classes: {missing}')

4. Feature Extraction¶

Four representations per segment:

Key Source Shape
chroma librosa CQT chroma (N_FRAMES, 12)
deep_chroma madmom DeepChromaProcessor (N_FRAMES, 12)
tonnetz Tonnetz from CQT chroma (N_FRAMES, 6)
tonnetz_deep Tonnetz from DeepChroma (N_FRAMES, 6)

Tonnetz dimensions: [P5 sin, P5 cos, m3 sin, m3 cos, M3 sin, M3 cos]

All segments are padded/truncated to N_FRAMES then z-score normalised per segment. For SMT, audio is sliced at .lab timestamps. For Beatles, pre-cut files are loaded whole. DeepChroma on SMT slices writes a temporary wav (madmom requires a file path).

In [94]:
from madmom.audio.chroma import DeepChromaProcessor
import soundfile as sf, tempfile

DCP = DeepChromaProcessor()

def fix_frames(arr: np.ndarray, n: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Pad or truncate (T, F) array to (n, F)."""
    T, F = arr.shape
    if T >= n:
        return arr[:n]
    return np.vstack([arr, np.zeros((n-T, F), dtype=arr.dtype)])

def normalise(arr: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Z-score normalise per feature dimension (axis=0 = across time)."""
    mean = arr.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)   # shape (1, F)
    std  = arr.std(axis=0, keepdims=True)    # shape (1, F)
    return (arr - mean) / (std + 1e-8)

def extract_features(filepath: str, start: float=None, end: float=None) -> dict:
    """Extract all four representations for one audio segment.
    start/end in seconds: used for SMT (long wav). None for Beatles (pre-cut).

    NOTE: fix_frames (pad/truncate) is applied BEFORE normalisation so that
    zero-padding doesn't corrupt the z-score statistics."""
    if start is not None:
        y, sr = librosa.load(filepath, sr=SR, mono=True,
                             offset=start, duration=end-start)
    else:
        y, sr = librosa.load(filepath, sr=SR, mono=True)

    # CQT chroma: (12,T) → (T,12)
    chroma = librosa.feature.chroma_cqt(y=y, sr=sr, hop_length=HOP, fmin=65, n_octaves=5).T

    # Tonnetz from CQT chroma: (6,T) → (T,6)
    tonnetz = librosa.feature.tonnetz(chroma=chroma.T).T

    # DeepChroma: madmom needs a file path, so write tmp wav for SMT slices
    if start is not None:
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.wav', delete=False) as tmp:
            tmp_path = tmp.name
        sf.write(tmp_path, y, SR)
        deep_chroma = DCP(tmp_path).astype(np.float32)   # (T,12)
        os.unlink(tmp_path)
    else:
        deep_chroma = DCP(filepath).astype(np.float32)   # (T,12)

    # Tonnetz from DeepChroma: (6,T) → (T,6)
    tonnetz_deep = librosa.feature.tonnetz(chroma=deep_chroma.T).T

    # fix_frames FIRST (pad/truncate to N_FRAMES), THEN normalise
    return {
        'chroma':       normalise(fix_frames(chroma,       N_FRAMES)),
        'deep_chroma':  normalise(fix_frames(deep_chroma,  N_FRAMES)),
        'tonnetz':      normalise(fix_frames(tonnetz,      N_FRAMES)),
        'tonnetz_deep': normalise(fix_frames(tonnetz_deep, N_FRAMES)),
    }

# Sanity check
_row = smt_df.iloc[0]
_f   = extract_features(_row['filepath'], _row['start'], _row['end'])
for k,v in _f.items():
    print(f'{k:15s}: shape={v.shape}  mean={v.mean():.4f}  std={v.std():.4f}')

chroma         : shape=(32, 12)  mean=-0.0000  std=0.9574
deep_chroma    : shape=(32, 12)  mean=0.0000  std=1.0000
tonnetz        : shape=(32, 6)  mean=-0.0000  std=1.0000
tonnetz_deep   : shape=(32, 6)  mean=0.0000  std=1.0000
In [95]:
def extract_and_cache(df: pd.DataFrame, cache_path: Path) -> dict:
    """Extract features for all rows in df, cache to .npz.
    For SMT rows: uses start/end columns to slice audio.
    For Beatles rows: loads whole pre-cut file."""
    if cache_path.exists():
        print(f'Loading cached features: {cache_path}')
        data = np.load(cache_path)
        return {k: data[k] for k in data.files}

    print(f'Extracting {len(df)} segments...')
    all_feats = {k: [] for k in REP_DIMS}
    labels, failed = [], []
    has_timestamps = 'start' in df.columns

    for _, row in tqdm(df.iterrows(), total=len(df)):
        try:
            start = row['start'] if has_timestamps else None
            end   = row['end']   if has_timestamps else None
            feats = extract_features(row['filepath'], start, end)
            for k in all_feats:
                all_feats[k].append(feats[k])
            labels.append(LABEL2IDX[row['label']])
        except Exception as e:
            failed.append((row['filepath'], str(e)))

    if failed:
        print(f'{len(failed)} files failed:')
        for fp, err in failed[:3]:
            print(f'  {fp}: {err}')

    result = {k: np.stack(v).astype(np.float32) for k, v in all_feats.items()}
    result['labels'] = np.array(labels, dtype=np.int64)
    np.savez(cache_path, **result)
    print(f'Saved to {cache_path}')
    return result


smt_data     = extract_and_cache(smt_df,     FEAT_DIR / 'smt_features.npz')
beatles_data = extract_and_cache(beatles_df, FEAT_DIR / 'beatles_features.npz')

print('\nShapes:')
for k, v in smt_data.items():
    print(f'  SMT     {k:15s}: {v.shape}')
for k, v in beatles_data.items():
    print(f'  Beatles {k:15s}: {v.shape}')

Loading cached features: /kaggle/working/features/smt_features.npz
Loading cached features: /kaggle/working/features/beatles_features.npz

Shapes:
  SMT     chroma         : (1908, 32, 12)
  SMT     deep_chroma    : (1908, 32, 12)
  SMT     tonnetz        : (1908, 32, 6)
  SMT     tonnetz_deep   : (1908, 32, 6)
  SMT     labels         : (1908,)
  Beatles chroma         : (1518, 32, 12)
  Beatles deep_chroma    : (1518, 32, 12)
  Beatles tonnetz        : (1518, 32, 6)
  Beatles tonnetz_deep   : (1518, 32, 6)
  Beatles labels         : (1518,)
In [96]:
# ── Utility for subsetting feature dicts ───────────────────────────────────────
def make_subset(feats, labels, mask):
    """Subset a feature dict by boolean mask."""
    sub = {}
    for k in feats:
        if k == 'labels':
            continue
        sub[k] = feats[k][mask]
    sub['labels'] = labels[mask]
    return sub

def concat_data(*dicts):
    """Concatenate multiple feature dicts."""
    # Get all non-label keys from first dict
    keys = [k for k in dicts[0] if k != 'labels']
    combined = {}
    for k in keys:
        combined[k] = np.concatenate([d[k] for d in dicts], axis=0)
    combined['labels'] = np.concatenate([d['labels'] for d in dicts])
    return combined

# ── Schubert feature extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────
schubert_data = extract_and_cache(schubert_df, FEAT_DIR / 'schubert_features.npz')

# Feature extraction may drop files (too short for DeepChroma).
# If cached data has fewer rows than schubert_df, filter to loadable files.
n_extracted = len(schubert_data['labels'])
n_original  = len(schubert_df)

if n_extracted < n_original:
    print(f'Note: {n_original - n_extracted} segments failed extraction.')
    print(f'Filtering to loadable files...')
    surviving = []
    for idx in range(n_original):
        row = schubert_df.iloc[idx]
        try:
            y, _ = librosa.load(row['filepath'], sr=SR, mono=True)
            if len(y) > HOP * 2:  # need at least a couple frames
                surviving.append(idx)
        except:
            continue
    surviving = surviving[:n_extracted]
    schubert_df = schubert_df.iloc[surviving].reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f'Aligned: {len(schubert_df)} rows match {n_extracted} extracted segments.')
else:
    schubert_df = schubert_df.reset_index(drop=True)

# Split features by piece mask using the aligned dataframe
schubert_train_mask = np.array([schubert_df.iloc[i]['piece'] <= piece_cutoff
                                for i in range(len(schubert_df))])
schubert_test_mask  = ~schubert_train_mask

schubert_train_data = make_subset(schubert_data, schubert_data['labels'], schubert_train_mask)
schubert_test_data  = make_subset(schubert_data, schubert_data['labels'], schubert_test_mask)

# Pooled real-world data
all_real_data = concat_data(beatles_data, schubert_data)

print(f'\nSchubert features:')
for k, v in schubert_data.items():
    print(f'  {k:15s}: {v.shape}')
print(f'  Train (pieces 1-{piece_cutoff}): {len(schubert_train_data["labels"])}')
print(f'  Test  (pieces {piece_cutoff+1}+):  {len(schubert_test_data["labels"])}')
print(f'\nAll real-world pooled: {len(all_real_data["labels"])} segments')

Loading cached features: /kaggle/working/features/schubert_features.npz
Note: 9 segments failed extraction.
Filtering to loadable files...
Aligned: 2155 rows match 2155 extracted segments.

Schubert features:
  chroma         : (2155, 32, 12)
  deep_chroma    : (2155, 32, 12)
  tonnetz        : (2155, 32, 6)
  tonnetz_deep   : (2155, 32, 6)
  labels         : (2155,)
  Train (pieces 1-12): 1243
  Test  (pieces 13+):  912

All real-world pooled: 3673 segments

4b. PCA Ablation — Per-Experiment Projection Fitting¶

The PCA ablation compares three 6-D projection methods against the 4 base representations:

Projection Description Data-dependent?
Tonnetz Fixed Harte (2006) geometry: circle of fifths + thirds No — same transform everywhere
PCA-6 Top 6 principal components of the 12-D chroma Yes — fitted on training data of each experiment
Random-6 Random orthonormal basis No — fixed random matrix

PCA is fitted on the training data of each experiment separately, then applied to both train and test. This ensures a fair comparison, Tonnetz uses no training data, so PCA shouldn't get an unfair advantage from seeing data outside the current experiment's training set.

The random projection uses a fixed orthonormal matrix (same across all experiments) as a null baseline: if random projections work as well as PCA, the specific PCA directions don't matter.

PCA Explained Variance¶

PCA variance is computed per-experiment within run_experiment below.

5. Dataset & DataLoaders¶

In [98]:
class ChordDataset(Dataset):
    """Wraps (N,T,F) feature array + (N,) label array.
    Adds channel dim: (N,T,F) → (N,1,T,F) for Conv2d."""
    def __init__(self, features: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray):
        self.X = torch.tensor(features[:, None, :, :], dtype=torch.float32)
        self.y = torch.tensor(labels, dtype=torch.long)
    def __len__(self):   return len(self.y)
    def __getitem__(self, i): return self.X[i], self.y[i]


def seed_worker(worker_id):
    np.random.seed(SEED + worker_id)
    import random; random.seed(SEED + worker_id)

g = torch.Generator()
g.manual_seed(SEED)

def make_loaders(data: dict, rep: str, val_split: float = VAL_SPLIT,
                 val_data: dict = None):
    """If val_data is provided, use it as the fixed validation set (no random split)."""
    if val_data is not None:
        train_ds = ChordDataset(data[rep], data['labels'])
        val_ds   = ChordDataset(val_data[rep], val_data['labels'])
    else:
        ds = ChordDataset(data[rep], data['labels'])
        n_val   = int(len(ds) * val_split)
        n_train = len(ds) - n_val
        train_ds, val_ds = random_split(ds, [n_train, n_val],
                                        generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(SEED))
    
    g = torch.Generator()          # fresh generator every call
    g.manual_seed(SEED)
    
    train_loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True,
                              num_workers=0, worker_init_fn=seed_worker, generator=g)
    val_loader   = DataLoader(val_ds, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False,
                              num_workers=0, worker_init_fn=seed_worker)
    return train_loader, val_loader

def make_test_loader(data: dict, rep: str):
    ds = ChordDataset(data[rep], data['labels'])
    return DataLoader(ds, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False,
                      num_workers=0)

6. CNN Architecture¶

Same architecture across all conditions, only input feature dimension differs (12 for chroma variants, 6 for Tonnetz/PCA/random variants). This isolates the effect of the representation.

Parameter count is identical (134,136) across all conditions.

In [99]:
class ChordCNN(nn.Module):
    """Input: (B, 1, N_FRAMES, n_features)  Output: (B, N_CLASSES) logits"""
    def __init__(self, n_features: int, n_classes: int=N_CLASSES):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(1,  32, kernel_size=(8,3), padding=(4,1)),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32), nn.ReLU())
        self.conv2 = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(32, 64, kernel_size=(4,3), padding=(2,1)),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(64), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(kernel_size=(2,1)))   # pool time only
        self.conv3 = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(64, 128, kernel_size=(4,3), padding=(2,1)),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(128), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(kernel_size=(2,1)))   # pool time only
        self.gap = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1,1))
        self.head = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Dropout(0.5),
            nn.Linear(128, 64), nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(64, n_classes))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv3(self.conv2(self.conv1(x)))
        return self.head(self.gap(x).flatten(1))

    def get_first_layer_filters(self):
        """Returns (n_filters, kH, kW) numpy array."""
        return self.conv1[0].weight.detach().cpu().numpy()[:, 0, :, :]

# Shape verification
for name, n in [('chroma',12),('tonnetz',6)]:
    m   = ChordCNN(n_features=n)
    out = m(torch.zeros(4,1,N_FRAMES,n))
    params = sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters())
    print(f'{name:12s}  input=(4,1,{N_FRAMES},{n})  output={tuple(out.shape)}  params={params:,}')

chroma        input=(4,1,32,12)  output=(4, 24)  params=134,136
tonnetz       input=(4,1,32,6)  output=(4, 24)  params=134,136

7. Training & Evaluation Utilities¶

In [ ]:
def train_model(rep: str, n_features: int, train_loader, val_loader,
                epochs: int=EPOCHS) -> tuple:
    """Train ChordCNN, return (best_model, history_dict)."""
    model     = ChordCNN(n_features=n_features).to(DEVICE)
    optimiser = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=LR, weight_decay=1e-4)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimiser, T_max=epochs)
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    history   = {'train_loss':[], 'val_loss':[], 'val_acc':[]}
    best_acc, best_state = 0.0, None

    for epoch in range(1, epochs+1):
        model.train()
        train_loss = 0.0
        for X, y in train_loader:
            X, y = X.to(DEVICE), y.to(DEVICE)
            optimiser.zero_grad()
            loss = criterion(model(X), y)
            loss.backward(); optimiser.step()
            train_loss += loss.item() * len(y)
        train_loss /= len(train_loader.dataset)

        model.eval(); val_loss = correct = 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for X, y in val_loader:
                X, y = X.to(DEVICE), y.to(DEVICE)
                logits = model(X)
                val_loss += criterion(logits, y).item() * len(y)
                correct  += (logits.argmax(1)==y).sum().item()
        val_loss /= len(val_loader.dataset)
        val_acc   = correct / len(val_loader.dataset)
        history['train_loss'].append(train_loss)
        history['val_loss'].append(val_loss)
        history['val_acc'].append(val_acc)
        scheduler.step()
        if val_acc > best_acc:
            best_acc   = val_acc
            best_state = {k: v.clone() for k,v in model.state_dict().items()}
        if epoch % 10 == 0:
            print(f'  epoch {epoch:3d}/{epochs}  '
                  f'train={train_loss:.4f}  val={val_loss:.4f}  acc={val_acc:.4f}')

    model.load_state_dict(best_state)
    return model, history


def evaluate(model: nn.Module, loader: DataLoader) -> tuple:
    """Return (preds, targets) as numpy arrays."""
    model.eval(); preds, targets = [], []
    with torch.no_grad():
        for X, y in loader:
            preds.extend(model(X.to(DEVICE)).argmax(1).cpu().numpy())
            targets.extend(y.numpy())
    return np.array(preds), np.array(targets)


# ── Evaluation metrics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Tonnetz distance matrix over 12 roots
def _build_tonnetz_dist():
    """Tonal centroid vectors per Harte et al. (2006) eq. 3.
    Radii: r1=r2=1 (fifths, minor thirds), r3=0.5 (major thirds)."""
    r = [1.0, 1.0, 0.5]
    def vec(l):
        return np.array([
            r[0] * np.sin(l * 7*np.pi/6),   r[0] * np.cos(l * 7*np.pi/6),   # P5
            r[1] * np.sin(l * 3*np.pi/2),   r[1] * np.cos(l * 3*np.pi/2),   # m3
            r[2] * np.sin(l * 2*np.pi/3),   r[2] * np.cos(l * 2*np.pi/3),   # M3
        ])
    vecs = np.stack([vec(i) for i in range(12)])
    D = np.zeros((12, 12))
    for i in range(12):
        for j in range(12):
            D[i, j] = np.linalg.norm(vecs[i] - vecs[j])
    return D

ROOT_DIST = _build_tonnetz_dist()

# Consonance vector
# Index = semitones, value = dissonance score (lower = more consonant)
# [P1, m2, M2, m3, M3, P4, TT, P5, m6, M6, m7, M7]
CONSONANCE_VEC = np.array([0,7,5,1,1,2,3,1,2,2,4,6], dtype=float)

def compute_metrics(preds, targets) -> dict:
    """Compute all evaluation metrics for a set of predictions."""
    acc       = (preds==targets).mean()
    macro_f1  = f1_score(targets, preds, average='macro',    zero_division=0)
    weighted_f1 = f1_score(targets, preds, average='weighted', zero_division=0)

    # Maj/min split
    maj_mask = np.array([IDX2LABEL[t].endswith('maj') for t in targets])
    min_mask = ~maj_mask
    _maj_labels = [i for i, l in IDX2LABEL.items() if l.endswith('maj')]
    _min_labels = [i for i, l in IDX2LABEL.items() if l.endswith('min')]
    maj_f1 = f1_score(targets[maj_mask], preds[maj_mask],
                      labels=[l for l in _maj_labels if l in set(targets[maj_mask])],
                      average='macro', zero_division=0)
    min_f1 = f1_score(targets[min_mask], preds[min_mask],
                      labels=[l for l in _min_labels if l in set(targets[min_mask])],
                      average='macro', zero_division=0)

    # TWE — Tonnetz-distance weighted error
    def twe_dist(p, t):
        ra, qa = IDX2LABEL[p].split(':'); rb, qb = IDX2LABEL[t].split(':')
        return ROOT_DIST[ROOTS.index(ra), ROOTS.index(rb)] + (0.0 if qa==qb else 0.5)
    twe = np.mean([twe_dist(int(p),int(t)) for p,t in zip(preds,targets)])

    # MCE — Mechanical-Consonance error
    def mce_dist(p, t):
        ra, qa = IDX2LABEL[p].split(':'); rb, qb = IDX2LABEL[t].split(':')
        ia, ib = ROOTS.index(ra), ROOTS.index(rb)
        interval = (ib - ia) % 12
        return float(CONSONANCE_VEC[interval]) + (0.0 if qa==qb else 1.0)
    mce = np.mean([mce_dist(int(p),int(t)) for p,t in zip(preds,targets)])

    return dict(acc=acc, macro_f1=macro_f1, weighted_f1=weighted_f1,
                maj_f1=maj_f1, min_f1=min_f1, twe=twe, mce=mce)

from sklearn.decomposition import PCA

# Fixed random orthonormal projections (same across all experiments — null baseline)
_rng = np.random.default_rng(SEED)
Q_CHROMA, _ = np.linalg.qr(_rng.standard_normal((12, 6)))
Q_DEEP,   _ = np.linalg.qr(_rng.standard_normal((12, 6)))

# Extended representation config (base 4 + PCA + random = 8 total)
ALL_REP_DIMS = {
    'chroma': 12, 'deep_chroma': 12, 'tonnetz': 6, 'tonnetz_deep': 6,
    'chroma_pca6': 6, 'deep_chroma_pca6': 6,
    'chroma_rand6': 6, 'deep_chroma_rand6': 6,
}
ALL_COLORS = {
    **COLORS,
    'chroma_pca6': '#9B59B6', 'deep_chroma_pca6': '#1ABC9C',
    'chroma_rand6': '#BDC3C7', 'deep_chroma_rand6': '#7F8C8D',
}

def add_projections(data, pca_chroma, pca_deep):
    """Add PCA and random projections to a feature dict in-place."""
    for src, dst, proj in [
        ('chroma',      'chroma_pca6',      lambda x: pca_chroma.transform(x)),
        ('deep_chroma', 'deep_chroma_pca6', lambda x: pca_deep.transform(x)),
        ('chroma',      'chroma_rand6',     lambda x: x @ Q_CHROMA),
        ('deep_chroma', 'deep_chroma_rand6',lambda x: x @ Q_DEEP),
    ]:
        N, T, F = data[src].shape
        data[dst] = proj(data[src].reshape(-1, F)).reshape(N, T, 6).astype(np.float32)


def run_experiment(train_feats, test_feats, exp_name, epochs=EPOCHS,
                   val_feats=None, verbose_pca=False):
    """Train all 8 representation variants, evaluate on test_feats.

    PCA is fitted on the TRAINING data of this experiment only,
    then applied to train, val, and test. This ensures a fair comparison
    with Tonnetz (which uses no training data).
    """
    # ── Step 1: Fit PCA on training data ──────────────────────────────────────
    pca_chroma = PCA(n_components=6, random_state=SEED).fit(
        train_feats['chroma'].reshape(-1, 12))
    pca_deep = PCA(n_components=6, random_state=SEED).fit(
        train_feats['deep_chroma'].reshape(-1, 12))

    if verbose_pca:
        print(f'  [{exp_name}] PCA explained variance:')
        print(f'    chroma:      {pca_chroma.explained_variance_ratio_.cumsum()[-1]:.3f}')
        print(f'    deep_chroma: {pca_deep.explained_variance_ratio_.cumsum()[-1]:.3f}')

    # ── Step 2: Apply projections to all data splits ──────────────────────────
    # Work on copies so we don't mutate the originals
    train_ext = {k: v.copy() if isinstance(v, np.ndarray) else v
                 for k, v in train_feats.items()}
    test_ext  = {k: v.copy() if isinstance(v, np.ndarray) else v
                 for k, v in test_feats.items()}
    add_projections(train_ext, pca_chroma, pca_deep)
    add_projections(test_ext,  pca_chroma, pca_deep)

    val_ext = None
    if val_feats is not None:
        val_ext = {k: v.copy() if isinstance(v, np.ndarray) else v
                   for k, v in val_feats.items()}
        add_projections(val_ext, pca_chroma, pca_deep)

    # ── Step 3: Train and evaluate all 8 representations ──────────────────────
    exp_res, exp_hist = {}, {}
    for rep, n_feat in ALL_REP_DIMS.items():
        print(f'  [{exp_name}] CNN-{rep}...')
        tl, vl = make_loaders(train_ext, rep, val_data=val_ext)
        model, hist = train_model(rep, n_feat, tl, vl, epochs=epochs)
        preds, targets = evaluate(model, make_test_loader(test_ext, rep))
        metrics = compute_metrics(preds, targets)
        exp_res[rep]  = {'preds': preds, 'targets': targets, **metrics}
        exp_hist[rep] = hist
        print(f'    acc={metrics["acc"]:.4f}  macro_f1={metrics["macro_f1"]:.4f}  '
              f'maj_f1={metrics["maj_f1"]:.4f}  min_f1={metrics["min_f1"]:.4f}')

    return exp_res, exp_hist


def summarise(exp_res, exp_name):
    rows = [{'Model': f'CNN-{r}', 'Accuracy': round(v['acc'], 4),
             'Macro F1': round(v['macro_f1'], 4), 'Maj F1': round(v['maj_f1'], 4),
             'Min F1': round(v['min_f1'], 4), 'TWE': round(v['twe'], 4),
             'MCE': round(v['mce'], 4)}
            for r, v in exp_res.items()]
    df = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values('Macro F1', ascending=False)
    print(f'\n{exp_name}')
    print(df.to_string(index=False))
    safe_name = exp_name.replace(' ', '_').replace('\u2192', 'to').replace('→', 'to')
    df.to_csv(str(OUT_DIR / f'{safe_name}.csv'), index=False)
    return df

def split_feats(feats, val_split=VAL_SPLIT, seed=SEED):
    """Split a feature dict into (train_feats, val_feats) by random index."""
    n = len(feats['labels'])
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    idx = rng.permutation(n)
    n_val = int(n * val_split)
    val_idx, train_idx = idx[:n_val], idx[n_val:]
    def subset(f, i):
        sub = {}
        for k in f:
            sub[k] = f[k][i]
        return sub
    return subset(feats, train_idx), subset(feats, val_idx)

8. Main Experiment: SMT → Beatles¶

In [101]:
# ── E1: SMT → Beatles (main experiment) ───────────────────────────────────────
print('E1: Train=SMT  Test=Beatles')
results, histories = run_experiment(smt_data, beatles_data, 'E1_SMT_to_Beatles',
                                    verbose_pca=True)
df_e1 = summarise(results, 'E1 SMT→Beatles')

# Store trained models for filter visualisation later
# (Re-train just the 3 we need for filter vis)
trained_models = {}
for rep in ['tonnetz', 'tonnetz_deep', 'deep_chroma_pca6']:
    print(f'  Re-training CNN-{rep} for filter visualisation...')
    # Need to build extended data with PCA for this
    pca_c = PCA(n_components=6, random_state=SEED).fit(smt_data['chroma'].reshape(-1, 12))
    pca_d = PCA(n_components=6, random_state=SEED).fit(smt_data['deep_chroma'].reshape(-1, 12))
    smt_ext = {k: v.copy() for k, v in smt_data.items()}
    add_projections(smt_ext, pca_c, pca_d)
    tl, vl = make_loaders(smt_ext, rep)
    model, _ = train_model(rep, ALL_REP_DIMS[rep], tl, vl)
    trained_models[rep] = model

  epoch  10/40  train=0.8261  val=1.1942  acc=0.6247
  epoch  20/40  train=0.4129  val=1.2296  acc=0.7060
  epoch  30/40  train=0.2638  val=0.5770  acc=0.8373
  epoch  40/40  train=0.2297  val=0.5348  acc=0.8583
    acc=0.2016  macro_f1=0.1714  maj_f1=0.2633  min_f1=0.1925
  [E1_SMT_to_Beatles] CNN-deep_chroma_pca6...
  epoch  10/40  train=0.5273  val=0.7371  acc=0.8215
  epoch  20/40  train=0.2410  val=0.5306  acc=0.8845
  epoch  30/40  train=0.1644  val=0.4518  acc=0.9081
  epoch  40/40  train=0.1149  val=0.4355  acc=0.9055
    acc=0.4433  macro_f1=0.3897  maj_f1=0.4952  min_f1=0.5094

  epoch  20/40  train=0.6478  val=1.1429  acc=0.6903

8a. Training curves¶

In [102]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1,2, figsize=(13,4))
for rep, hist in histories.items():
    axes[0].plot(hist['val_loss'], label=f'CNN-{rep}', color=ALL_COLORS.get(rep, '#333'))
    axes[1].plot(hist['val_acc'],  label=f'CNN-{rep}', color=ALL_COLORS.get(rep, '#333'))
axes[0].set_title('Validation Loss'); axes[0].set_xlabel('Epoch')
axes[1].set_title('Validation Accuracy (SMT val)'); axes[1].set_xlabel('Epoch')
for ax in axes:
    ax.legend(fontsize=7); ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'training_curves.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

8b. Results table¶

In [103]:
summary = pd.DataFrame([{
    'Model':        f'CNN-{rep}',
    'Input Dim':    ALL_REP_DIMS[rep],
    'Accuracy':     round(results[rep]['acc'],          4),
    'Macro F1':     round(results[rep]['macro_f1'],     4),
    'Maj F1':       round(results[rep]['maj_f1'],       4),
    'Min F1':       round(results[rep]['min_f1'],       4),
    'TWE':          round(results[rep]['twe'],          4),
    'MCE':          round(results[rep]['mce'],          4),
} for rep in ALL_REP_DIMS]).sort_values('Macro F1', ascending=False)

print(summary.to_string(index=False))
summary.to_csv(str(OUT_DIR / 'main_results.csv'), index=False)

8c. Summary bar chart¶

In [104]:
reps   = list(ALL_REP_DIMS.keys())
labels = [f'CNN-{r}' for r in reps]
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1,3, figsize=(16,4))

for ax, key, title, ylabel in zip(axes,
    ['acc',     'macro_f1',             'min_f1'],
    ['Accuracy','Macro F1',             'Minor-class Macro F1'],
    ['Accuracy','Macro F1',             'Macro F1 (minor only)']):
    vals = [results[r][key] for r in reps]
    bars = ax.bar(labels, vals, color=[ALL_COLORS.get(r,'#333') for r in reps],
                  edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
    ax.set_title(title, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel)
    ax.set_ylim(0,1)
    for bar, v in zip(bars, vals):
        ax.text(bar.get_x()+bar.get_width()/2, v+0.01, f'{v:.3f}',
                ha='center', fontsize=7)
    ax.set_xticklabels(labels, rotation=20, ha='right', fontsize=7)
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Main Results: SMT → Beatles (out-of-domain)',
             fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'main_results_bar.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

8c-bis. Projection method comparison — the ablation headline¶

This chart answers: does the Tonnetz geometry help, or is any 6-D projection good enough?

In [105]:
# ── Projection method comparison (headline ablation result) ────────────────────
proj_groups = {
    'CQT Chroma base': {
        'Tonnetz':  'tonnetz',
        'PCA-6':    'chroma_pca6',
        'Random-6': 'chroma_rand6',
        '12-D':     'chroma',
    },
    'DeepChroma base': {
        'Tonnetz':  'tonnetz_deep',
        'PCA-6':    'deep_chroma_pca6',
        'Random-6': 'deep_chroma_rand6',
        '12-D':     'deep_chroma',
    },
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4.5))
for ax, (group_name, methods) in zip(axes, proj_groups.items()):
    names = list(methods.keys())
    reps_  = list(methods.values())
    f1_vals  = [results[r]['macro_f1'] for r in reps_]
    acc_vals = [results[r]['acc']      for r in reps_]

    x = np.arange(len(names))
    w = 0.35
    b1 = ax.bar(x - w/2, acc_vals, w, label='Accuracy',
                color=[ALL_COLORS[r] for r in reps_], edgecolor='black', lw=0.5, alpha=0.6)
    b2 = ax.bar(x + w/2, f1_vals, w, label='Macro F1',
                color=[ALL_COLORS[r] for r in reps_], edgecolor='black', lw=0.5)

    for bar, v in zip(b1, acc_vals):
        ax.text(bar.get_x()+bar.get_width()/2, v+0.01, f'{v:.3f}',
                ha='center', fontsize=7, color='grey')
    for bar, v in zip(b2, f1_vals):
        ax.text(bar.get_x()+bar.get_width()/2, v+0.01, f'{v:.3f}',
                ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels(names, fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel('Score')
    ax.set_ylim(0, 0.7)
    ax.set_title(group_name, fontweight='bold', fontsize=11)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Projection Method Comparison — SMT → Beatles',
             fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'projection_comparison.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

print('\nKey finding: PCA-6 > Tonnetz in BOTH chroma families.')
print('Data-driven dimensionality reduction outperforms the hand-crafted geometric prior.')

Key finding: PCA-6 > Tonnetz in BOTH chroma families.
Data-driven dimensionality reduction outperforms the hand-crafted geometric prior.

8d. Musically-aware error metrics¶

TWE (Tonnetz-Weighted Error): penalises misclassifications by geometric Tonnetz distance between roots.

MCE (Mechanical-Consonance Error): penalises by perceptual dissonance score from the consonance vector. Lower = better for both.

In [106]:
# Visualise consonance vector
interval_names = ['P1','m2','M2','m3','M3','P4','TT','P5','m6','M6','m7','M7']
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9,3))
bar_colors = ['#2ecc71' if v<=1 else '#e67e22' if v<=3 else '#e74c3c'
              for v in CONSONANCE_VEC]
bars = ax.bar(interval_names, CONSONANCE_VEC, color=bar_colors,
              edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
for bar,v in zip(bars, CONSONANCE_VEC):
    ax.text(bar.get_x()+bar.get_width()/2, v+0.05, str(int(v)),
            ha='center', fontsize=9)
ax.set_title('Consonance Vector vt — lower = more consonant',
             fontweight='bold')
ax.set_ylabel('Dissonance score'); ax.set_xlabel('Interval')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'consonance_vector.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

# TWE and MCE comparison
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1,2, figsize=(11,4))
for ax, key, title, ylabel in zip(axes,
    ['twe',                    'mce'],
    ['Tonnetz-Weighted Error', 'Mechanical-Consonance Error'],
    ['Mean Tonnetz Distance',  'Mean Consonance Penalty']):
    vals = [results[r][key] for r in ALL_REP_DIMS]
    bars = ax.bar(labels, vals, color=[ALL_COLORS.get(r, "#333") for r in ALL_REP_DIMS],
                  edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
    ax.set_title(f'{title} (↓ better)', fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel)
    for bar,v in zip(bars,vals):
        ax.text(bar.get_x()+bar.get_width()/2, v+max(vals)*0.01, f'{v:.3f}',
                ha='center', fontsize=8)
    ax.set_xticklabels(labels, rotation=15, ha='right', fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.suptitle('Musically-Aware Error Metrics — SMT → Beatles',
             fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'musical_error_metrics.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

8e. Confusion matrices (Beatles test)¶

In [131]:
# ── Focused confusion matrices: best, mid-tier, worst + difference ────────────
focus_reps = ['deep_chroma_pca6', 'tonnetz_deep', 'chroma']
focus_labels = ['Best (deep_chroma_pca6)', 'Mid (tonnetz_deep)', 'Worst (chroma)']

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(20, 4.5))

# Individual CMs for 3 key models
cms_norm = {}
for ax, rep, label in zip(axes[:3], focus_reps, focus_labels):
    cm      = confusion_matrix(results[rep]['targets'], results[rep]['preds'])
    cm_n = cm.astype(float) / cm.sum(axis=1, keepdims=True).clip(min=1)
    cms_norm[rep] = cm_n
    sns.heatmap(cm_n, ax=ax, cmap='Blues', vmin=0, vmax=1,
                xticklabels=CHORD_LABELS, yticklabels=CHORD_LABELS,
                cbar=True, square=True)
    ax.set_title(f'{label}\nacc={results[rep]["acc"]:.3f}', fontweight='bold', fontsize=9)
    ax.set_xlabel('Predicted'); ax.set_ylabel('True')
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=5)
    plt.setp(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=90)
    plt.setp(ax.get_yticklabels(), rotation=0)


plt.suptitle('Confusion Matrices — Best vs Mid vs Worst', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'confusion_matrices_focused.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

8f. Per-root × per-quality accuracy heatmap¶

In [108]:
# ── Per-root × per-quality accuracy heatmap — top 4 models only ───────────────
focus_reps_4 = ['deep_chroma_pca6', 'deep_chroma_rand6', 'tonnetz_deep', 'deep_chroma']
n = len(focus_reps_4)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, n, figsize=(4*n, 5))
for ax, rep in zip(axes, focus_reps_4):
    matrix = np.array([
        [(results[rep]['preds'][results[rep]['targets']==LABEL2IDX[f'{r}:{q}']] ==
          LABEL2IDX[f'{r}:{q}']).mean()
         if (results[rep]['targets']==LABEL2IDX[f'{r}:{q}']).sum() > 0
         else np.nan
         for q in QUALITIES] for r in ROOTS
    ])  # (12,2)
    im = ax.imshow(matrix.T, aspect='auto', cmap='RdYlGn', vmin=0, vmax=1)
    ax.set_xticks(range(12)); ax.set_xticklabels(ROOTS, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_yticks(range(2));  ax.set_yticklabels(QUALITIES, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title(f'CNN-{rep}\n(acc={results[rep]["acc"]:.3f})', fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Root')
    for i in range(12):
        for j in range(2):
            v = matrix[i,j]
            if not np.isnan(v):
                ax.text(i, j, f'{v:.2f}', ha='center', va='center',
                        fontsize=7, color='black' if 0.25<v<0.8 else 'white')
    plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, shrink=0.7)
plt.suptitle('Per-root × Per-quality Accuracy — Top 4 Models (Beatles test)',
             fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'per_root_accuracy_focused.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

8g. Major vs minor macro F1 — quality gap analysis¶

In [109]:
print('Major vs Minor Macro F1:')

print('-'*50)
for rep in ALL_REP_DIMS:
    mj = results[rep]['maj_f1']; mn = results[rep]['min_f1']
    print(f'CNN-{rep:<16s}  {mj:8.4f}  {mn:8.4f}  {mj-mn:8.4f}')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9,4))
x   = np.arange(len(ALL_REP_DIMS)); w = 0.35
ax.bar(x-w/2, [results[r]['maj_f1'] for r in ALL_REP_DIMS], w,
       label='Major', color=[ALL_COLORS.get(r, '#333') for r in ALL_REP_DIMS], edgecolor='black', lw=0.5)
ax.bar(x+w/2, [results[r]['min_f1'] for r in ALL_REP_DIMS], w,
       label='Minor', color=[ALL_COLORS.get(r, '#333') for r in ALL_REP_DIMS], edgecolor='black', lw=0.5,
       hatch='//')

                                      rotation=15, ha='right')
ax.set_ylabel('Macro F1'); ax.set_ylim(0,1)
ax.set_title('Major vs Minor Macro F1 — Beatles test', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(); ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'maj_min_f1.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

Major vs Minor Macro F1:
Model                   Maj F1    Min F1       Gap
--------------------------------------------------
CNN-chroma              0.1376    0.1864   -0.0488
CNN-deep_chroma         0.3328    0.2498    0.0830
CNN-tonnetz             0.3087    0.3399   -0.0312
CNN-tonnetz_deep        0.4683    0.4292    0.0392
CNN-chroma_pca6         0.2633    0.1925    0.0708
CNN-deep_chroma_pca6    0.4952    0.5094   -0.0142
CNN-chroma_rand6        0.1979    0.1790    0.0189
CNN-deep_chroma_rand6    0.3931    0.4448   -0.0517

8g-bis. Error-type decomposition: root vs quality errors¶

For each misclassification, we decompose into three categories: correct root but wrong quality, wrong root but correct quality, or both wrong. This directly tests whether Tonnetz/PCA helps quality discrimination.

In [110]:
# ── Error-type decomposition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
def error_decomposition(preds, targets):
    """Decompose errors into root-only, quality-only, and both-wrong."""
    correct = root_err = qual_err = both_err = 0
    for p, t in zip(preds, targets):
        p_root, p_qual = IDX2LABEL[p].split(':')
        t_root, t_qual = IDX2LABEL[t].split(':')
        if p == t:
            correct += 1
        elif p_root == t_root and p_qual != t_qual:
            qual_err += 1   # correct root, wrong quality
        elif p_root != t_root and p_qual == t_qual:
            root_err += 1   # wrong root, correct quality
        else:
            both_err += 1   # both wrong
    n = len(preds)
    return {'correct': correct/n, 'quality_err': qual_err/n,
            'root_err': root_err/n, 'both_err': both_err/n}

decomp = {rep: error_decomposition(results[rep]['preds'], results[rep]['targets'])
           for rep in ALL_REP_DIMS}

# Sort by macro F1 for clearer visual
sorted_reps = sorted(ALL_REP_DIMS.keys(), key=lambda r: results[r]['macro_f1'], reverse=True)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 5))
x = np.arange(len(sorted_reps))
w = 0.6
bottoms = np.zeros(len(sorted_reps))

err_types = [
    ('correct',     '#2ecc71', 'Correct'),
    ('quality_err', '#f39c12', 'Root ✓, Quality ✗'),
    ('root_err',    '#3498db', 'Root ✗, Quality ✓'),
    ('both_err',    '#e74c3c', 'Both wrong'),
]
for key, color, label in err_types:
    vals = [decomp[r][key] for r in sorted_reps]
    ax.bar(x, vals, w, bottom=bottoms, label=label, color=color,
           edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.5)
    # Label segments > 5%
    for i, v in enumerate(vals):
        if v > 0.05:
            ax.text(x[i], bottoms[i] + v/2, f'{v:.0%}',
                    ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7, fontweight='bold')
    bottoms += vals

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels([f'CNN-{r}' for r in sorted_reps], rotation=20, ha='right', fontsize=8)
ax.set_ylabel('Proportion of test samples')
ax.set_title('Error Decomposition — SMT → Beatles', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=8)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.05)
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'error_decomposition.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

print('\nError decomposition (sorted by Macro F1):')
print(f'{"Model":<25s}  {"Correct":>8s}  {"Qual Err":>8s}  {"Root Err":>8s}  {"Both":>8s}')
print('-'*65)
for rep in sorted_reps:
    d = decomp[rep]
    print(f'CNN-{rep:<21s}  {d["correct"]:8.1%}  {d["quality_err"]:8.1%}  '
          f'{d["root_err"]:8.1%}  {d["both_err"]:8.1%}')

Error decomposition (sorted by Macro F1):
Model                       Correct  Qual Err  Root Err      Both
-----------------------------------------------------------------
CNN-deep_chroma_pca6          44.3%      7.4%     21.4%     26.8%
CNN-tonnetz_deep              41.2%      5.3%     22.1%     31.4%
CNN-deep_chroma_rand6         37.4%      7.5%     26.7%     28.4%
CNN-deep_chroma               29.1%      5.5%     47.0%     18.4%
CNN-tonnetz                   25.8%     17.1%     23.2%     33.9%
CNN-chroma_pca6               20.2%     12.3%     33.5%     34.0%
CNN-chroma_rand6              15.4%     12.8%     29.4%     42.4%
CNN-chroma                    14.5%     10.3%     33.6%     41.6%

8. Filter Visualisation¶

In [112]:
def _is_quality_filter(fdata, thresh_frac=0.4):
    p = fdata.mean(axis=0)
    m3 = p[2]+p[3]; M3 = p[4]+p[5]
    t  = thresh_frac * np.abs(p).max()
    return (abs(m3)>t and abs(M3)>t and np.sign(m3)!=np.sign(M3))

def _is_fifth_filter(fdata, thresh_frac=0.4):
    p  = fdata.mean(axis=0)
    p5 = p[0]+p[1]; m3 = p[2]+p[3]; M3 = p[4]+p[5]
    t  = thresh_frac * np.abs(p).max()
    return (abs(p5)>t and abs(p5)>abs(m3) and abs(p5)>abs(M3))
In [128]:
# ── Labelled filter grids — Tonnetz models only + deep_chroma_pca6 comparison ─

from matplotlib.patches import FancyArrowPatch
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec

TONNETZ_AXIS_LABELS = ['5th sin','5th cos','m3 sin','m3 cos','M3 sin','M3 cos']
TONNETZ_AXIS_COLORS = ['#2166ac','#2166ac','#d6604d','#d6604d','#1a9850','#1a9850']

def compute_full_sensitivity(filters, n_feat=6, kernel_w=3):
    n_pos    = n_feat - kernel_w + 1
    full     = np.zeros((filters.shape[0], filters.shape[1], n_feat))
    for pos in range(n_pos):
        full[:, :, pos:pos+kernel_w] += filters
    return full

# Show only interpretable models: tonnetz, tonnetz_deep, and deep_chroma_pca6 for comparison
FILTER_VIS_REPS = ['tonnetz', 'tonnetz_deep', 'deep_chroma_pca6']

for rep in FILTER_VIS_REPS:
    filters  = trained_models[rep].get_first_layer_filters()   # (32, 8, 3)
    is_tz    = 'tonnetz' in rep

    if is_tz:
        plot_data = compute_full_sensitivity(filters, n_feat=6, kernel_w=3)
        feat_labels = TONNETZ_AXIS_LABELS
        feat_colors = TONNETZ_AXIS_COLORS
        feat_desc   = 'Full 6-dim Tonnetz sensitivity (accumulated over sliding positions)'
    else:
        plot_data = compute_full_sensitivity(filters, n_feat=6, kernel_w=3)
        feat_labels = [f'PC{i+1}' for i in range(6)]
        feat_colors = ['#555555'] * 6
        feat_desc   = 'PCA-6 dimensions (data-driven, not directly interpretable)'

    vmax = np.abs(plot_data).max()
    ncols_grid = 8
    nrows_grid = 4

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(
        nrows_grid, ncols_grid,
        figsize=(ncols_grid * 2.0, nrows_grid * 2.8),
        gridspec_kw={'hspace': 0.55, 'wspace': 0.35}
    )

    for idx, ax in enumerate(axes.flatten()):
        fdata = plot_data[idx]
        im = ax.imshow(fdata.T, aspect='auto',
                       cmap='RdBu_r', vmin=-vmax, vmax=vmax,
                       interpolation='nearest')
        ax.set_title(f'F{idx+1}', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold', pad=3)
        ax.set_xticks([]); ax.set_yticks([])

        if idx % ncols_grid == 0:
            ax.set_yticks(range(len(feat_labels)))
            ax.set_yticklabels(feat_labels, fontsize=5.5)
            for tick, color in zip(ax.get_yticklabels(), feat_colors):
                tick.set_color(color)

        if is_tz:
            profile = fdata.mean(axis=0)
            m3_score = float(profile[2] + profile[3])
            M3_score = float(profile[4] + profile[5])
            p5_score = float(profile[0] + profile[1])
            max_abs   = np.abs(profile).max()
            thresh    = 0.4 * max_abs
            opponent  = (abs(m3_score) > thresh and abs(M3_score) > thresh
                         and np.sign(m3_score) != np.sign(M3_score))
            fifth_dom = (abs(p5_score) > thresh
                         and abs(p5_score) > abs(m3_score)
                         and abs(p5_score) > abs(M3_score))


    cbar_ax = fig.add_axes([0.92, 0.15, 0.015, 0.7])
    sm = plt.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap='RdBu_r',
                               norm=plt.Normalize(vmin=-vmax, vmax=vmax))
    sm.set_array([])
    cb = fig.colorbar(sm, cax=cbar_ax)
    cb.set_label('Filter weight', fontsize=8)
    cb.ax.tick_params(labelsize=7)



    fig.suptitle(
        f'CNN-{rep} — First Layer Filters\n'
        f'Y-axis: {feat_desc}\n'
        f'X-axis: 8 time frames (~180ms)  |  '
        f'Red=excitatory  Blue=inhibitory  White=insensitive',
        fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', y=1.01
    )

    plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / f'filters_labelled_{rep}.png'),
                dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
    plt.show()
    print(f'CNN-{rep}:  mean|w|={np.abs(filters).mean():.4f}  '
          f'max|w|={np.abs(filters).max():.4f}')

CNN-tonnetz:  mean|w|=0.1005  max|w|=0.2986

CNN-tonnetz_deep:  mean|w|=0.1032  max|w|=0.2484

CNN-deep_chroma_pca6:  mean|w|=0.1070  max|w|=0.2798

9. Cross-Dataset Experiment Matrix¶

Seven transfer experiments spanning three corpora (SMT synth, Beatles pop, Schubert classical) to test whether representation rankings are stable across musical domains.

# Train Test Tests
E1 SMT Beatles Synth → pop
E2 Beatles SMT Pop → synth (reverse)
E3 SMT Schubert (SC06) Synth → classical
E4 Schubert (SC06) SMT Classical → synth
E5 SMT All real (Beatles + Schubert) Synth → mixed real
E6 All real (Beatles + Schubert) SMT Mixed real → synth
E7 SMT + Schubert-train + Revolver Schubert-test + Sgt Pepper's Mixed holdout (cross-genre)

Data isolation in E7: Schubert is split by piece number (pieces 1–12 train, 13–24 test), Beatles by album (Revolver train, Sgt Pepper's test). No segment appears in both train and test.

E2: Beatles → SMT (reverse transfer)¶

In [114]:
print('E2: Train=Beatles  Test=SMT')
exp_e2_res, _ = run_experiment(beatles_data, smt_data, 'E2_Beatles_to_SMT')
df_e2 = summarise(exp_e2_res, 'E2 Beatles→SMT')

E5: SMT → All real-world (Beatles + Schubert)¶

Tests generalisation from synth to a heterogeneous real-world mix spanning pop and classical.

In [117]:
print('E5: Train=SMT  Test=All real (Beatles + Schubert)')
exp_e5_res, _ = run_experiment(smt_data, all_real_data, 'E5_SMT_to_AllReal')
df_e5 = summarise(exp_e5_res, 'E5 SMT→AllReal')

E6: All real-world → SMT (reverse)¶

In [118]:
print('E6: Train=All real (Beatles + Schubert)  Test=SMT')
exp_e6_res, _ = run_experiment(all_real_data, smt_data, 'E6_AllReal_to_SMT')
df_e6 = summarise(exp_e6_res, 'E6 AllReal→SMT')

E7: Mixed holdout — cross-genre generalisation¶

Train: SMT (full) + Schubert pieces 1–12 + Beatles Revolver Test: Schubert pieces 13–24 + Beatles Sgt Pepper's

Data isolation: No Schubert piece or Beatles album appears in both train and test. SMT is fully synthetic and shares no recordings with either real-world set.

In [119]:
# ── E7: Mixed holdout ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Beatles split by album
revolver_mask   = np.array(['revolver'   in beatles_df.iloc[i]['filepath'].lower()
                             for i in range(len(beatles_df))])
sgtpepper_mask  = ~revolver_mask
beatles_revolver = make_subset(beatles_data, beatles_data['labels'], revolver_mask)
beatles_sgtpepper = make_subset(beatles_data, beatles_data['labels'], sgtpepper_mask)

print(f'Beatles split: Revolver={revolver_mask.sum()}  Sgt Peppers={sgtpepper_mask.sum()}')
print(f'Schubert split: Train (pieces 1-{piece_cutoff})={len(schubert_train_data["labels"])}  '
      f'Test (pieces {piece_cutoff+1}+)={len(schubert_test_data["labels"])}')

# Train: SMT + Schubert-train + Revolver
# Use SMT val split for validation (clean, no test leakage)
smt_train_e7, smt_val_e7 = split_feats(smt_data)
mixed_train_e7 = concat_data(smt_train_e7, schubert_train_data, beatles_revolver)

# Test: Schubert-test + Sgt Pepper's
mixed_test_e7 = concat_data(schubert_test_data, beatles_sgtpepper)

print(f'E7 Train: {len(mixed_train_e7["labels"])} '
      f'(SMT-train {len(smt_train_e7["labels"])} + '
      f'Schubert-train {len(schubert_train_data["labels"])} + '
      f'Revolver {revolver_mask.sum()})')
print(f'E7 Val:   {len(smt_val_e7["labels"])} (SMT val only)')
print(f'E7 Test:  {len(mixed_test_e7["labels"])} '
      f'(Schubert-test {len(schubert_test_data["labels"])} + '
      f'Sgt Peppers {sgtpepper_mask.sum()})')

print('\nE7: Mixed holdout — cross-genre')
exp_e7_res, _ = run_experiment(mixed_train_e7, mixed_test_e7,
                               'E7_MixedHoldout', val_feats=smt_val_e7)
df_e7 = summarise(exp_e7_res, 'E7 Mixed Holdout (cross-genre)')

Beatles split: Revolver=678  Sgt Peppers=840
Schubert split: Train (pieces 1-12)=1243  Test (pieces 13+)=912
E7 Train: 3448 (SMT-train 1527 + Schubert-train 1243 + Revolver 678)
E7 Val:   381 (SMT val only)
E7 Test:  1752 (Schubert-test 912 + Sgt Peppers 840)

10. Cross-Experiment Summary¶

In [127]:
all_experiments = {
    'E1: SMT\u2192Beatles':      results,
    'E2: Beatles\u2192SMT':      exp_e2_res,
    'E3: SMT\u2192Schubert':     exp_e3_res,
    'E4: Schubert\u2192SMT':     exp_e4_res,
    'E5: SMT\u2192AllReal':      exp_e5_res,
    'E6: AllReal\u2192SMT':      exp_e6_res,
    'E7: Mixed holdout':    exp_e7_res,
}

# ── Macro F1 summary table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
rows = []
for exp_name, exp_res in all_experiments.items():
    row = {'Experiment': exp_name}
    for rep in ALL_REP_DIMS:
        row[f'CNN-{rep}'] = round(exp_res[rep]['macro_f1'], 4)
    row['Best'] = max(ALL_REP_DIMS, key=lambda r: exp_res[r]['macro_f1'])
    rows.append(row)

summary_all = pd.DataFrame(rows)
print('MACRO F1 ACROSS ALL EXPERIMENTS')
print('='*110)
print(summary_all.to_string(index=False))
summary_all.to_csv(str(OUT_DIR / 'all_experiments_macro_f1.csv'), index=False)

# ── Multi-metric rank stability heatmaps ──────────────────────────────────────
# Show separate rank matrices for Macro F1, TWE, and MCE
rank_metrics = [
    ('macro_f1',  'Macro F1 (\u2191 better)', False),
    ('twe',       'TWE (\u2193 better)',       True),
    ('mce',       'MCE (\u2193 better)',       True),
]

exp_names = list(all_experiments.keys())
rep_list  = list(ALL_REP_DIMS.keys())

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(22, 6), gridspec_kw={'right': 0.92})

for ax, (metric_key, metric_title, lower_is_better) in zip(axes, rank_metrics):
    rank_matrix = np.zeros((len(exp_names), len(rep_list)))
    for i, (exp_name, exp_res) in enumerate(all_experiments.items()):
        scores = [exp_res[r][metric_key] for r in rep_list]
        if lower_is_better:
            order = np.argsort(scores)  # ascending = best first for lower-is-better
        else:
            order = np.argsort(scores)[::-1]  # descending = best first for higher-is-better
        ranks = np.zeros(len(rep_list))
        for rank_pos, idx in enumerate(order):
            ranks[idx] = rank_pos + 1
        rank_matrix[i] = ranks

    im = ax.imshow(rank_matrix, cmap='RdYlGn_r', vmin=1, vmax=len(rep_list), aspect='auto')

    ax.set_xticks(range(len(rep_list)))
    ax.set_xticklabels([f'CNN-{r}' for r in rep_list], rotation=35, ha='right', fontsize=7)
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(exp_names)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(exp_names if ax == axes[0] else [], fontsize=8)

    for i in range(len(exp_names)):
        for j in range(len(rep_list)):
            rank = int(rank_matrix[i, j])
            color = 'white' if rank <= 2 or rank >= 7 else 'black'
            fontw = 'bold' if rank == 1 else 'normal'
            ax.text(j, i, str(rank), ha='center', va='center',
                    fontsize=9, color=color, fontweight=fontw)

    ax.set_title(metric_title, fontweight='bold', fontsize=10)

plt.colorbar(im, ax=axes.tolist(), label='Rank (1=best)', shrink=0.6, pad=0.03)
plt.suptitle('Rank Stability Across Experiments — by Metric',
             fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'rank_stability_multi_metric.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

# ── Winner summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print('\nWinner per experiment (Macro F1 | TWE | MCE):')
print(f'{"Experiment":<25s}  {"Macro F1 winner":<22s}  {"TWE winner":<22s}  {"MCE winner":<22s}')
print('-'*95)
for exp_name, exp_res in all_experiments.items():
    best_f1  = max(ALL_REP_DIMS, key=lambda r: exp_res[r]['macro_f1'])
    best_twe = min(ALL_REP_DIMS, key=lambda r: exp_res[r]['twe'])
    best_mce = min(ALL_REP_DIMS, key=lambda r: exp_res[r]['mce'])
    print(f'{exp_name:<25s}  CNN-{best_f1:<18s}  CNN-{best_twe:<18s}  CNN-{best_mce:<18s}')

# ── Domain breakdown ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print('\n--- Domain comparison (by Macro F1) ---')
pop_exps = ['E1: SMT\u2192Beatles', 'E2: Beatles\u2192SMT']
classical_exps = ['E3: SMT\u2192Schubert', 'E4: Schubert\u2192SMT']
mixed_exps = ['E5: SMT\u2192AllReal', 'E6: AllReal\u2192SMT', 'E7: Mixed holdout']

for domain, exps in [('Pop (E1-E2)', pop_exps),
                     ('Classical (E3-E4)', classical_exps),
                     ('Mixed (E5-E7)', mixed_exps)]:
    mean_f1 = {}
    for rep in ALL_REP_DIMS:
        vals = [all_experiments[e][rep]['macro_f1'] for e in exps]
        mean_f1[rep] = np.mean(vals)
    best = max(mean_f1, key=mean_f1.get)
    print(f'  {domain:<25s}: best avg = CNN-{best} ({mean_f1[best]:.4f})')

MACRO F1 ACROSS ALL EXPERIMENTS
==============================================================================================================
       Experiment  CNN-chroma  CNN-deep_chroma  CNN-tonnetz  CNN-tonnetz_deep  CNN-chroma_pca6  CNN-deep_chroma_pca6  CNN-chroma_rand6  CNN-deep_chroma_rand6             Best
  E1: SMT→Beatles      0.1098           0.2555       0.2209            0.3537           0.1714                0.3897            0.1326                 0.3215 deep_chroma_pca6
  E2: Beatles→SMT      0.0877           0.1909       0.2654            0.7205           0.1265                0.5508            0.1697                 0.5238     tonnetz_deep
 E3: SMT→Schubert      0.1027           0.1116       0.2147            0.2239           0.2011                0.1871            0.1352                 0.1603     tonnetz_deep
 E4: Schubert→SMT      0.0843           0.1019       0.4123            0.6889           0.1734                0.4532            0.2430                 0.4119     tonnetz_deep
  E5: SMT→AllReal      0.1097           0.2012       0.2340            0.3111           0.1998                0.2899            0.1343                 0.2515     tonnetz_deep
  E6: AllReal→SMT      0.1596           0.4558       0.5796            0.8722           0.2213                0.7431            0.3234                 0.6153     tonnetz_deep
E7: Mixed holdout      0.1892           0.3612       0.3430            0.5060           0.1578                0.4050            0.1909                 0.3717     tonnetz_deep

Winner per experiment (Macro F1 | TWE | MCE):
Experiment                 Macro F1 winner         TWE winner              MCE winner            
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
E1: SMT→Beatles            CNN-deep_chroma_pca6    CNN-deep_chroma_pca6    CNN-deep_chroma_pca6  
E2: Beatles→SMT            CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep      
E3: SMT→Schubert           CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz             CNN-chroma_pca6       
E4: Schubert→SMT           CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep      
E5: SMT→AllReal            CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-deep_chroma_pca6    CNN-tonnetz           
E6: AllReal→SMT            CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep      
E7: Mixed holdout          CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep        CNN-tonnetz_deep      

--- Domain comparison (by Macro F1) ---
  Pop (E1-E2)              : best avg = CNN-tonnetz_deep (0.5371)
  Classical (E3-E4)        : best avg = CNN-tonnetz_deep (0.4564)
  Mixed (E5-E7)            : best avg = CNN-tonnetz_deep (0.5631)

10a. Error decomposition across experiments¶

Decomposing misclassifications into root errors, quality errors, and both-wrong.

In [121]:
# ── Error decomposition across ALL experiments ────────────────────────────────
all_exp_for_decomp = {
    'E1: SMT→Beatles':   results,
    'E3: SMT→Schubert':  exp_e3_res,
    'E5: SMT→AllReal':   exp_e5_res,
    'E7: Mixed holdout': exp_e7_res,
}

focus_reps = ['deep_chroma_pca6', 'tonnetz_deep', 'deep_chroma', 'tonnetz']

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(all_exp_for_decomp), figsize=(5*len(all_exp_for_decomp), 5))

for ax, (exp_name, exp_res) in zip(axes, all_exp_for_decomp.items()):
    decomps = {}
    for rep in focus_reps:
        decomps[rep] = error_decomposition(exp_res[rep]['preds'], exp_res[rep]['targets'])

    x = np.arange(len(focus_reps))
    w = 0.6
    bottoms = np.zeros(len(focus_reps))

    err_types = [
        ('correct',     '#2ecc71', 'Correct'),
        ('quality_err', '#f39c12', 'Quality ✗'),
        ('root_err',    '#3498db', 'Root ✗'),
        ('both_err',    '#e74c3c', 'Both ✗'),
    ]
    for key, color, label in err_types:
        vals = [decomps[r][key] for r in focus_reps]
        ax.bar(x, vals, w, bottom=bottoms, label=label, color=color,
               edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.5)
        for i, v in enumerate(vals):
            if v > 0.05:
                ax.text(x[i], bottoms[i] + v/2, f'{v:.0%}',
                        ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7, fontweight='bold')
        bottoms += vals

    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels([f'CNN-{r}' for r in focus_reps], rotation=25, ha='right', fontsize=7)
    ax.set_ylabel('Proportion')
    ax.set_title(exp_name, fontweight='bold', fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1.05)
    if ax == axes[-1]:
        ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=7)

plt.suptitle('Error Decomposition Across Experiments', fontweight='bold', fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'error_decomp_all_experiments.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()

# Print table
print(f'\n{"Experiment":<22s} {"Model":<22s} {"Correct":>8s} {"QualErr":>8s} {"RootErr":>8s} {"Both":>8s}')
print('-'*75)
for exp_name, exp_res in all_exp_for_decomp.items():
    for rep in focus_reps:
        d = error_decomposition(exp_res[rep]['preds'], exp_res[rep]['targets'])
        print(f'{exp_name:<22s} CNN-{rep:<18s} {d["correct"]:8.1%} {d["quality_err"]:8.1%} '
              f'{d["root_err"]:8.1%} {d["both_err"]:8.1%}')
    print()

Experiment             Model                   Correct  QualErr  RootErr     Both
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
E1: SMT→Beatles        CNN-deep_chroma_pca6      44.3%     7.4%    21.4%    26.8%
E1: SMT→Beatles        CNN-tonnetz_deep          41.2%     5.3%    22.1%    31.4%
E1: SMT→Beatles        CNN-deep_chroma           29.1%     5.5%    47.0%    18.4%
E1: SMT→Beatles        CNN-tonnetz               25.8%    17.1%    23.2%    33.9%

E3: SMT→Schubert       CNN-deep_chroma_pca6      20.6%     5.2%    36.9%    37.2%
E3: SMT→Schubert       CNN-tonnetz_deep          25.2%     6.0%    35.0%    33.8%
E3: SMT→Schubert       CNN-deep_chroma           15.3%     3.5%    50.4%    30.8%
E3: SMT→Schubert       CNN-tonnetz               22.0%     9.5%    29.7%    38.7%

E5: SMT→AllReal        CNN-deep_chroma_pca6      30.6%     5.6%    35.0%    28.8%
E5: SMT→AllReal        CNN-tonnetz_deep          31.8%     4.9%    30.9%    32.5%
E5: SMT→AllReal        CNN-deep_chroma           22.3%     4.1%    48.8%    24.8%
E5: SMT→AllReal        CNN-tonnetz               24.6%    12.9%    27.6%    34.9%

E7: Mixed holdout      CNN-deep_chroma_pca6      50.1%     6.1%    21.2%    22.6%
E7: Mixed holdout      CNN-tonnetz_deep          59.9%     6.7%    16.7%    16.7%
E7: Mixed holdout      CNN-deep_chroma           43.4%     7.6%    25.9%    23.1%
E7: Mixed holdout      CNN-tonnetz               40.0%    10.6%    27.1%    22.4%

10b. Comprehensive metric comparison (F1 variants + musical error metrics)¶

Comparing Macro F1, Micro F1, Weighted F1, TWE, and MCE across experiments tests whether the ranking is robust to the choice of evaluation metric.

In [122]:
# ── Comprehensive metrics comparison across all experiments ───────────────────
def compute_full_metrics(preds, targets):
    acc       = (preds == targets).mean()
    macro_f1  = f1_score(targets, preds, average='macro',    zero_division=0)
    micro_f1  = f1_score(targets, preds, average='micro',    zero_division=0)
    weighted_f1 = f1_score(targets, preds, average='weighted', zero_division=0)
    def twe_dist(p, t):
        ra, qa = IDX2LABEL[p].split(':'); rb, qb = IDX2LABEL[t].split(':')
        return ROOT_DIST[ROOTS.index(ra), ROOTS.index(rb)] + (0.0 if qa == qb else 0.5)
    twe = np.mean([twe_dist(int(p), int(t)) for p, t in zip(preds, targets)])
    def mce_dist(p, t):
        ra, qa = IDX2LABEL[p].split(':'); rb, qb = IDX2LABEL[t].split(':')
        ia, ib = ROOTS.index(ra), ROOTS.index(rb)
        interval = (ib - ia) % 12
        return float(CONSONANCE_VEC[interval]) + (0.0 if qa == qb else 1.0)
    mce = np.mean([mce_dist(int(p), int(t)) for p, t in zip(preds, targets)])
    return dict(acc=acc, macro_f1=macro_f1, micro_f1=micro_f1,
                weighted_f1=weighted_f1, twe=twe, mce=mce)

exp_dict = {
    'E1: SMT→Beatles':   results,
    'E3: SMT→Schubert':  exp_e3_res,
    'E5: SMT→AllReal':   exp_e5_res,
    'E7: Mixed holdout': exp_e7_res,
}

all_exp_metrics = {}
for exp_name, exp_res in exp_dict.items():
    all_exp_metrics[exp_name] = {}
    for rep in focus_reps:
        all_exp_metrics[exp_name][rep] = compute_full_metrics(
            exp_res[rep]['preds'], exp_res[rep]['targets'])

# Print tables
metrics_list = ['macro_f1', 'micro_f1', 'weighted_f1', 'twe', 'mce']
metric_labels = {'macro_f1': 'Macro F1', 'micro_f1': 'Micro F1',
                 'weighted_f1': 'Wt. F1', 'twe': 'TWE ↓', 'mce': 'MCE ↓'}

for exp_name in exp_dict:
    print(f'\n{"="*85}')
    print(f'{exp_name}')
    print(f'{"="*85}')
    header = f'{"Model":<22s}'
    for m in metrics_list:
        header += f'  {metric_labels[m]:>10s}'
    print(header)
    print('-'*85)
    for rep in focus_reps:
        d = all_exp_metrics[exp_name][rep]
        row = f'CNN-{rep:<18s}'
        for m in metrics_list:
            row += f'  {d[m]:10.4f}'
        print(row)

# Winner matrix
print('\n\nWINNER MATRIX (metric × experiment):')
exp_short = ['SMT→Beatles', 'SMT→Schubert', 'SMT→AllReal', 'Mixed Holdout']
print(f'{"Metric":<15s}', end='')
for e in exp_short:
    print(f'  {e:>12s}', end='')
print()
print('-'*70)
for m in metrics_list:
    print(f'{metric_labels[m]:<15s}', end='')
    for exp_name in exp_dict:
        if 'twe' in m or 'mce' in m:
            best = min(focus_reps, key=lambda r: all_exp_metrics[exp_name][r][m])
        else:
            best = max(focus_reps, key=lambda r: all_exp_metrics[exp_name][r][m])
        short = best.replace('deep_chroma_', 'dc_').replace('tonnetz', 'tz')
        print(f'  {short:>12s}', end='')
    print()

In [123]:
# ── Musical error metrics across experiments ──────────────────────────────────
exp_names_short = ['SMT\u2192Beatles\n(pop)', 'SMT\u2192Schubert\n(classical)', 'SMT\u2192All Real\n(pop+classical)', 'Mixed Holdout\n(cross-genre)']
x = np.arange(len(exp_names_short))
w = 0.18

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
for ax, metric, title in zip(axes, ['twe', 'mce'],
    ['Tonnetz-Weighted Error (↓ better)', 'Mechanical-Consonance Error (↓ better)']):
    for i, rep in enumerate(focus_reps):
        vals = [all_exp_metrics[e][rep][metric] for e in exp_dict]
        ax.bar(x + i*w, vals, w, label=f'CNN-{rep}', color=ALL_COLORS[rep],
               edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
        for j, v in enumerate(vals):
            ax.text(x[j] + i*w, v + 0.02, f'{v:.2f}', ha='center', fontsize=6, rotation=45)
    ax.set_xticks(x + w*1.5)
    ax.set_xticklabels(exp_names_short, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel(metric.upper())
    ax.set_title(title, fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend(fontsize=7)
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.suptitle('Musical Error Metrics Across Transfer Experiments', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(str(OUT_DIR / 'musical_errors_all_experiments.png'), dpi=150); plt.show()
In [124]:
# ── F1 score variants across experiments ──────────────────────────────────────
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(16, 5))
f1_metrics = ['macro_f1', 'micro_f1', 'weighted_f1']
f1_titles = ['Macro F1 (class-balanced)', 'Micro F1 (sample-balanced)', 'Weighted F1']

for ax, metric, title in zip(axes, f1_metrics, f1_titles):
    for i, rep in enumerate(focus_reps):
        vals = [all_exp_metrics[e][rep][metric] for e in exp_dict]
        ax.bar(x + i*w, vals, w, label=f'CNN-{rep}', color=ALL_COLORS[rep],
               edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
    ax.set_xticks(x + w*1.5)
    ax.set_xticklabels(exp_names_short, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel(metric.replace('_', ' ').title())
    ax.set_title(title, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_ylim(0, 0.75)
    ax.legend(fontsize=7)
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
